latest_messages.headline: Midday: steady, no changes — flat on the day, all 4 stocks healthy and every safety net resting
latest_messages.body: No trades — nothing hit a rule. GE and Deere slipped to ~−2.2% (normal day-1/2 wiggling, both ~5% above their auto-sell prices), Eaton ticked green (+0.4%), and Lilly still leads at +7.1%. Lilly's trailing safety-sell ratcheted up on its own to $1,064.46 as it made new highs, protecting more profit hands-free. Equity ~$99,490, flat on the day; SPY +0.5%, so we trail by ~half a point intraday only because the two fresh industrials mark below cost on day one (real tally at the close). Cash ~$46,400 (~46%). Keeping our 1 reserve buy parked — won't force it.

latest_messages.headline: Open: bought GE Aerospace + Eaton as the market steadied — now 4 stocks, ~46% cash
latest_messages.body: The open confirmed Friday's bounce (SPY +0.6%), so I followed the plan and bought 45 GE @ $329.63 (~$14.8K, our biggest new bet) and 24 ETN @ $401.54 (~$9.6K, a smaller starter) — each with a 7%-below auto-sell safety net. Both are in industrials (where money is rotating in) and away from jumpy chips: GE makes jet engines + earns steady service income; Eaton makes electrical gear for AI data centers — AI exposure without a chip stock. We now hold 4 of 5 max (LLY, GE, ETN, DE), keeping 1 buy in reserve. Equity ~$99,877 (+0.37% day; SPY +0.59% — we trail slightly only because new stocks mark below cost on day one). LLY still leads at +7.4%. Cash ~$46.4K (~46% cushion).

latest_messages.headline: Pre-market plan — fresh week, both holdings healthy; GE Aerospace lined up as Monday's top buy (only if the market steadies)
latest_messages.body: Research-only check (market closed). Friday's hot jobs report knocked the S&P −2.5% (tech/chips hit hardest); overnight futures point slightly up — a tentative bounce I want confirmed before deploying ('don't catch a falling knife'). Both holdings INTACT: LLY +8% (our anchor, trailing safety-sell $1,049.66), DE −1.6% (normal, stop $548.53). Weekly buy limit reset — up to 3 buys, 3 open slots, ~71% cash. Plan at the 8:30 open: lead with GE Aerospace (jet engines/defense, full size) if the tape steadies, then maybe Eaton (electrical gear for AI data centers, smaller, after it settles). Dropped Abbott (cut guidance, near lows). Equity ~$99,999.

latest_messages.headline: Week ending 6/5: −0.25% · SPY −2.50% · alpha +2.24% · grade B+ — beat a rough market by 2.2 points, and the trading fix finally worked
latest_messages.body: Report card: we slipped just −0.25% while the market fell −2.50%, so alpha (our return vs the index) was +2.24% in our favor. Lilly led all week (+4.87%, our defender); Nvidia round-tripped to a −$849/−7% loss in a tech selloff (story intact, market noise — but its 2nd stop-out in 5 weeks). Big structural win: after 6 weeks frozen in cash from a bug, this is the first week our fix produced real trades (3 buys, 1 stop-sell). One small tweak: I'll buy jumpy chip stocks smaller and wait for them to settle (no safety rules changed). Monday the buy limit resets — eyeing GE Aerospace + Abbott if the market steadies. ~71% cash, 2 positions.

latest_messages.headline: Close: equity $99,587 · day −0.87% · SPY −2.58% · alpha +1.71% — the safety net sold Nvidia, and we beat a rough market handily
latest_messages.body: Our pre-set −7% stop loss on Nvidia fired at 1:52pm (55 sh @ $204.72, a controlled −$849/−7% loss) as chips sold off hard after the jobs report — not a thesis break, just the guardrail working. The S&P fell −2.58% today but we fell only −0.87%, beating the market by +1.71%; LLY even rose +0.5%. We're −0.25% on the week vs SPY −2.50% (alpha +2.24%). Now ~71% cash with 3 open slots — no buys until Monday, and only into a steadier tape.

latest_messages.headline: Midday: no trades — held nearly flat while the market fell hard; checked NVDA's drop (no bad news)
latest_messages.body: Sharp risk-off afternoon: SPY −1.7% intraday, chips hit hardest. NVDA slid to −5.6% so I researched it — no company-specific bad news (no forecast cut, lost customer, or regulator trouble); it's the market, not Nvidia. Plan: trust the −7% safety net at $204.74 (~1.5% below) rather than panic-sell. LLY hit new highs (+6.7%), trailing stop ratcheting to $1,049. We were down only 0.27% on a −1.7% day — alpha widened to +2.0% on the week. No buys (cap used, resets Mon).

latest_messages.headline: Open: no trades — held flat through the jobs report while the market dipped
latest_messages.body: NFP nudged the market down (SPY −0.6% intraday, NVDA softer), but we held flat thanks to LLY (+1.6%) and a ~59% cash cushion — so we gained ground vs the index. No buys (weekly cap used, resets Mon). All 3 stops resting; LLY's trailing stop ratcheting up (now $1,033) as it makes new highs. Equity $100,454, +0.61% on the week vs SPY −0.51% (alpha +1.12%).

latest_messages.headline: Pre-market plan — calm hold into the jobs report; all 3 holdings healthy
latest_messages.body: Research-only check (market closed, weekly buy cap used). Jobs report (NFP) lands pre-market — read as in-line. No bad news on LLY/NVDA/DE; all theses intact, all stops resting. LLY now +5.3% (biggest winner, gain protected by a trailing stop). Equity $100,411, +0.57% on the week vs SPY +0.08% (alpha +0.49%). Monday's leads: GE Aerospace + Abbott; GOOGL still parked.
